observations.recommendation:
1.15 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
b) Require the Inventory Committee to prepare the Report on the Physical Count
of PPE (RPCPPE) grouped per account for easy monitoring.
c.) Require the Municipal Accountant and the Property Custodian to submit an
explanation to the Audit Team why Property Ledger Cards and Property
Cards for all PPE accounts, respectively, were not maintained.
1.16 We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant reclassify the items
which are below the P15,000.00 threshold and to recognize the items found
during the count which were not yet recorded in the books.
1.17 Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the
Property Officer, reconcile their records, pursuant to COA Circular No.
2020-006 (One-Time Cleansing of PPE account balance) to arrive at a
reliable PPE balance as of yearend; and
1.18 Finally, we recommended that henceforth, the Inventory Committee conduct
a complete physical count of all properties of the LGU every year and to
prepare a completely filled out RPCPPE.

2.13 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant recognize the donated
ambulance and the corresponding depreciation in the LGU books.
2.14 We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Property Officer
to immediately file notice to the Insurance Company for the claim of covered
repairs of the unit in accordance with the provisions of the Insurance Policy.

3.10 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:
d) the focal person for the relief distribution to submit immediately the
Distribution Sheets to the Supply Officer;
e) the Supply Officer to consolidate the supplies and materials issued and
submit the SSMI to the Municipal Accountant;
f) the Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting entries to record the
issuances in order to correct the balances of the affected accounts.
3.11 Moving forward, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant and
Supply Officer prepare and maintain Supply Ledger Cards and Stock Cards,
respectively, and reconcile their records.

5.10 We recommended that the Municipal Health Officer submit the RSMI for all
procured and donated medicines within five days after end of each month to the
office of the Municipal Accountant which will be the basis for recording the
issuance in the books, to avoid overstatement of the inventory account.
5.11 We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Municipal
Accountant should reconcile its record with the RHU and adjust the balance
of the Drugs and Medicine for Distribution account accordingly.

6.9 We recommended that the Local DRRM Officer submit a justification, for
the Audit Team’s review and evaluation, why expenditures indicated in
Table 8 should be charged to LDRRMF.
6.10 Moving forward, we recommended:
a) Local DRRM Officer prepare an updated or revised LDRRMFIP to
reflect any changes in the PPAs to be implemented during the year;
and
b) MDRRM Officer and Budget Officer refrain from charging to the
Local DRRM Fund expenditures not related to disaster risk reduction
and management.

7.10 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor submit justification why
procurements were made through cash advance of the Municipal Treasurer
and not through direct issuance of check to supplier.
7.11 We recommended that the BAC Chairperson submit justification for the
Audit Team’s evaluation why materials for the Water System was not
procured through public bidding; and submit the aforementioned lacking
documents as required by laws, rules and regulations.
7.12 We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit justification
why disbursements were approved as liquidation despite the absence of the
appropriate supporting documents.
7.13 Management agreed to comply the recommendations. Cash advances for 2nd
semester was no longer practiced.

8.12 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Supply Officer to
comply the requirements for monitoring of fuel consumption and the
Municipal Accountant to review the completeness of the attached supporting
documents as required under COA Circular No. 77-61 or the Manual on Fuel
Consumption.

9.7 We recommended that management refrain from granting of overtime pay for
work that can be done during office hours and adhere to 5.10 of COA Circular
No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, Annex “C” of COA Circular No. 2012-003
dated October 29, 2012 and should strictly observe Civil Service Commission
– Department of Budget and Management (CSC-DBM) Joint Circular No. 2,
Series of 2015 dated November 25, 2015.

10.10 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately all DVs
with its supporting documents pertaining to the period January
2018 to December 2021 with its supporting documents to the
Audit Team.
b) Issue a written policy concerning on-time submission of accounts
by the Disbursing Officer to the Accounting Section and from
the Municipal Accountant to the Audit Team.

3. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the remaining 1,970 pieces water meters as inventory and adjust the recorded expense account accordingly.
4. We also recommended that the Municipal Engineer should:
a. Store the remaining water meters to a clean and safe stockroom to avoid possible deterioration;
b. Maintain a stock card for easy monitoring of the receipt and issuance of water meters; and
c. Submit a Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) duly supported with Requisition Issue Slip (RIS) on the issuance of water meter to the office of the Municipal Accountant for recording.
observations.managementAction:
Implemented
Implemented
Implemented
Implemented

observations.managementAction: Implemented

10. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the unreported collections and deposits in the books of accounts.

observations.recommendation:
15. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Treasurers submit written explanation to the Audit Team why payment on the transaction was made through cash advance during the 2nd semester of the year.
16. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit justification why vouchers were processed and indorsed for payment despite the lack of the required documents.
17. Moving forward, we recommended management to comply with pertinent requirements before payment is made to avoid audit suspensions.

Henceforth, utilize the BGCM exclusively for COVID-19 related projects, programs and activities as enumerated under LBC No. 125.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

22. We also recommended that the amount be refunded by the recipient, without prejudice to the joint and solidary liability of those who authorized and certified the legality and propriety of the payment.
23. We further recommended that henceforth, the concerned municipal officials should only approve transactions which are duly authorized by law to avoid disallowance in audit.

We also recommended that the Municipal Assessor coordinate with the PENRO Biliran for assistance on the steps to be taken for the titling of lands owned by LGU Cabucgayan through LAMP.

observations.recommendation: 36. Strictly require the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to conduct reconciliations of cash vault balances per cashbooks and ledgers at least weekly, the former to likewise comply with weekly turn-over of documents as well as reports covering consummated transactions to make possible the updating of records by the former. This has to be succeeded with recording of adjustments by the official whose records are considered erroneous on the basis of amount reflected per source documents like disbursement vouchers and payrolls with their supporting documents and their corresponding reports.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliations of cash vault balances per cashbooks and ledgers at least weekly is on-going. The former to likewise comply with weekly turn-over of documents as well as reports covering consummated transactions to make possible the updating of records by the former. This has to be succeeded with recording of adjustments by the official whose records are considered erroneous on the basis of amount reflected per source documents like disbursement vouchers and payrolls with their supporting documents and their corresponding reports.
observations.reason: Reconciliatio n is on-going.

observations.recommendation:
1.6. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile its record with
the RHU and adjust accordingly the balance of the Drugs and Medicine for
Distribution account.
1.7. We also recommended that the Municipal Health Officer submit the SSMI
for all procured and donated medicines within five days after end of each
month to the office of the Municipal Accountant which will be the basis for
recording the issuance.

2.10. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the remaining 1,970
pieces water meters as inventory and adjust the recorded expense account
accordingly.
2.11. We also recommended that the Municipal Engineer should:
a) Store the remaining water meters to a clean and safe stockroom to avoid
possible deterioration;
b) Maintain a stock card for easy monitoring of the receipt and issuance of
water meters; and
c) Submit a Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) duly
supported with Requisition Issue Slip (RIS) on the issuance of water
meter to the office of the Municipal Accountant for recording.
2.12. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor provide a stockroom for all
supplies and materials and consider the hiring of a Supply Officer to
maintain the stockroom.

3.7. Moving forward, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant record
the payment of honoraria of assigned national government personnel to
Other Professional Services account.

4.7. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare an adjusting
journal entry to record the above transaction to Property, Plant and
Equipment and provide its corresponding depreciation.

5.7. We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer record the daily
transactions in the Cashbook and reconcile with the Municipal Accountant’s
record every end of month in order to detect errors and effect corrections to
arrive at a correct balance of the account.

6.8. We recommended that the previous Municipal Treasurer submit
immediately to the Municipal Accountant the Report of Collections and
Deposits covering the missing Official Receipts.
6.9. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the unreported
collections and deposits in the books of accounts.

7.6. We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
d. Require all accountable officer /field collectors to remit their
collections to the Municipal Treasurer daily to avoid accumulation of funds
in their custody;
e. Deposit the collections intact and on time and prepare a report and
submit to the Municipal Accountant for recording in the books; and
f. Require the accountable officers a written explanation why deposits were
delayed and refer them to the Municipal Mayor for appropriate action.

8.6. We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer direct the collectors to
prepare the monthly Report on Accountability for Accountable Forms for all
accountable forms with and without money value in their custody in order to
monitor properly their receipt, issuances and balances for the month.

9.8. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Treasurer
submit written explanation to the Audit Team why payment on the
transaction was made through cash advance.
9.9. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit justification
why vouchers were processed and indorsed for payment despite the lack of
the required documents.
9.10. Moving forward, we recommended management to comply with pertinent
requirements before payment is made to avoid audit suspensions.

10.6. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Budget Officer
and end-user submit a valid justification for evaluation of the Audit Team
why these expenses be charged to BGCM. Henceforth, utilize the BGCM
exclusively for COVID-19 related projects, programs and activities as
enumerated under LBC No. 125.

11.7. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer
to submit the lacking Disbursement Vouchers and its supporting documents
to the Municipal Accountant for recording in the books of accounts.
11.8. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately
the subject Disbursement Vouchers to the Audit Team, otherwise a Notice of
Suspension or Disallowance will be issued. Thereafter, ensure that the
submission of financial reports and its attachments be made within the
prescribe period.

12.6. We recommended that management submit legal basis for the grant of
honoraria to the Election Officer, otherwise, stop the payment of the same to
the subject employee.
12.7. We also recommended that the amount be refunded by the recipient, without
prejudice to the joint and solidary liability of those who authorized and
certified the legality and propriety of the payment.
12.8. We further recommended that henceforth, the concerned municipal officials
should only approve transactions which are duly authorized by law to avoid
disallowance in audit.

2. record the Road Lot component of the municipal roads; and
3. maintain property ledger cards for each road complete with its components to support the recorded amount of the road network and to arrive at an accurate balance of the account.

observations.recommendation:
11. submit the lacking DVs totaling P4,787,784.90 within one month from receipt of this AOM to avoid sanctions for non-rendition of accounts; and
12. submit, without fail, on or before the 5th day of each month to the Municipal Accountant all paid disbursement vouchers and other financial transactions for recording purposes; and
13. inform and furnish the Municipal Accountant, with supporting documents like bank Credit Memos, or other supporting documents like fund transfer advice, whenever funds are transferred from the National Government Agencies, for recording purposes.
We also reiterate our prior year’s recommendations that
14. demand, in writing, from the Municipal Treasurer, the submission of disbursements vouchers and reports, if not submitted within the prescribed period;
15. record immediately in the books all transactions of the LGU within the period mandated by regulations;
16. submit the lacking DVs and its supporting documents to the Audit Team; and
17. prepare regularly a separate Bank Reconciliation Statement for each bank account of the LGU.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Treasurer:
11. submit the lacking DVs totaling P4,787,784.90 within one month from receipt of this AOM to avoid sanctions for non-rendition of accounts; and
12. submit, without fail, on or before the 5th day of each month to the Municipal Accountant all paid disbursement vouchers and other financial transactions for recording purposes; and
13. inform and furnish the Municipal Accountant, with supporting documents like bank Credit Memos, or other supporting documents like fund transfer advice, whenever funds are transferred from the National Government Agencies, for recording purposes.
14. demand, in writing, from the Municipal Treasurer, the submission of disbursements vouchers and reports, if not submitted within the prescribed period;
15. record immediately in the books all transactions of the LGU within the period mandated by regulations;
16. submit the lacking DVs and its supporting documents to the Audit Team; and
17. prepare regularly a separate Bank Reconciliation Statement for each bank account of the LGU.

observations.recommendation: 18. We recommended that in the subsequent preparation of Financial Statements, the Municipal Accountant present the current portion of Loans Payable – Domestic as a separate line item under Current Liabilities in the Statement of Financial Position for a better understanding of the financial status of the Municipality and the amount due to be paid in the succeeding year.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant present the current portion of Loans Payable – Domestic as a separate line item under Current Liabilities in the Statement of Financial Position.

observations.recommendation: 19. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare an adjusting entry to reduce the balance of the payable account, Due to LGU, by cancel the Due from LGU receivable in the Due to LGU account by the same amount.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepare an adjusting entry to reduce the balance of the payable account, Due to LGU, by cancel the Due from LGU receivable in the Due to LGU account by the same amount.

observations.recommendation:
20. include only in the LGU’s Annual Investment Plan projects which are permanent or capital in nature so that utilization of the 20% Fund will be for projects which will contribute to long-term socio-economic progress of the Municipality; and
21. review the contents of previous AIP to obtain the possible re-appropriations to those in the AIP no longer necessary and viable, so that all projects will be implemented during the year.
observations.managementAction: Include only in the LGU’s Annual Investment Plan projects which are permanent or capital in nature so that utilization of the 20% Fund will be for projects which will contribute to long-term socio-economic progress of the Municipality; review the contents of previous AIP to obtain the possible re-appropriations to those in the AIP no longer necessary and viable.

observations.recommendation:
22. We recommended that the Property Officer, with the assistance of the Municipal DRRM Officer, shall assist in the conduct of actual physical count of properties procured out of LDRRMF and prepare the Inventory List pertaining thereto including the identification of the persons accountable thereof.
23. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain an updated subsidiary ledgers on DRRM equipment and reconcile said records monthly with the records of the MDRRMO.
observations.managementAction: The Property Officer, with the assistance of the Municipal DRRM Officer, shall assist in the conduct of actual physical count of properties procured out of LDRRMF and prepare the Inventory List pertaining thereto including the identification of the persons accountable thereof. The Municipal Accountant maintain an updated subsidiary ledgers on DRRM equipment and reconcile said records monthly with the records of the MDRRMO.

Review RPT/SET collections of prior years up to CY 2019 to determine the shares of each Barangay of LGU-Cabucgayan and the Provincial share; prepare the Disbursement Vouchers for the prior years’ payables on RPT/SET shares to the recipient barangays and to the Province; prepare a monthly schedule showing the amount of the RPT/SET payable to the Province and the recipient LGUs and record in individual subsidiary ledger; prepare, within five (5) days after the end of each quarter, the Disbursement Vouchers for the release of the RPT/SET shares to the recipient barangays and to the Province.

observations.recommendation: 30. We recommended that the designated Disbursing Officer maintain a cash book for her cash advances for special operating expenses of the Municipality and to liquidate her previous cash advances before obtaining additional funds.
observations.managementAction: The designated Disbursing Officer will maintain a cash book for her cash advances for special operating expenses of the Municipality and will liquidate her previous cash advances before obtaining additional funds.

observations.recommendation: 31. We recommended that the OIC Municipal Treasurer initiate the preparation of a Contract of Lease for market stallholders while record of stallholders, payments for stall rentals were to be maintained and monitored, and renewals done every year. Also, maintain records of stallholder to monitor the collections from the market stalls.
observations.managementAction: The OIC Municipal Treasurer will initiate the preparation of a Contract of Lease for market stallholders and maintain records of stallholders, payments for stall rentals to monitor the collections from the market stalls.

observations.recommendation:
32. review previous procurement on PPE to obtain the necessary data about the properties;
33. prepare and maintain PPE Ledger Cards, and
34. identify and determine the discrepancies between the RPCPPE and Accounting Records.
35. Lastly, we recommended that the designated Property Officer prepare and maintain Property Custodian Cards for every property procured by the LGU and reconcile his records with Accounting Records monthly.
observations.managementAction: Review previous procurement on PPE to obtain the necessary data about the properties; prepare and maintain PPE Ledger Cards; identify and determine the discrepancies between the RPCPPE and Accounting Records; designated Property Officer to prepare and maintain Property Custodian Cards for every property procured by the LGU and reconcile his records with Accounting Records monthly.

submitted.
Not
implemented in full as noted.

observations.recommendation:
1.8. We recommended that the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Accountant
update, tally and harmonize their respective records so that the complete
description and cost segregation of each road component of the road projects
will be included in the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network.

1.9. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant: (a) record the Road
Lot component of the municipal roads; and (b) maintain property ledger cards
for each road complete with its components to support the recorded amount of
the road network and to arrive at an accurate balance of the account.

2.8. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant: (a) compute the correct
depreciation on the Road Network components by not deducting a residual
value thereon and including the depreciation from the year of its acquisition to
the current year; (b) prepare lapsing schedule for depreciation for each road
project and per road network component separately; and (c) prepare the
property ledger card for each road project.

3.7. We recommended that the Municipal Assessor:
3.7.1. start the process on the titling of lands owned by the Municipality of
Biliran to establish its ownership;
3.7.2. submit to the Municipal Treasurer copies of the TCT for custody and
safekeeping, and;
3.7.3. submit to the Accountant the list of parcels of land with TCT.

3.8. We also recommended that the Municipal Assessor coordinate with the
Provincial Environment and National Resources Officer (PENRO) Biliran for
assistance on the steps to be taken for the titling of lands owned by LGU
Cabucgayan through the Land Administration Management Project (LAMP).

4.7. We reiterate our prior year’s recommendations that the Municipal Treasurer:
4.7.1. submit the lacking DVs totaling P4,787,784.90 within one month from
receipt of this AOM to avoid sanctions for non-rendition of accounts;
4.7.2. submit, without fail, on or before the 5th day of each month to the
Municipal Accountant all paid disbursement vouchers and other
financial transactions for recording purposes; and
4.7.3. inform and furnish the Municipal Accountant, with bank Credit
Memos, or other supporting documents like fund transfer advice,
whenever funds are transferred from the National Government
Agencies, for recording purposes.

4.8. We also reiterate our prior year’s recommendations that the Municipal
Accountant:
4.8.1. demand, in writing, from the Municipal Treasurer, the submission of
disbursements vouchers and reports, if not submitted within the
prescribed period;
4.8.2. record immediately in the books all transactions of the LGU within
the period mandated by regulations;
4.8.3. submit the lacking DVs and its supporting documents to the Audit
Team; and
4.8.4. prepare regularly a separate Bank Reconciliation Statement for each
bank account of the LGU.

5.5. We recommended that in the subsequent preparation of Financial Statements,
the Municipal Accountant present the current portion of Loans Payable –
Domestic as a separate line item under Current Liabilities in the Statement of
Financial Position for a better understanding of the financial status of the
Municipality and the amount due to be paid in the succeeding year.

6.5. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare an adjusting entry
to reduce the balance of the payable account, Due from LGU, by P205, 772.41
and cancel the receivable in the Due to LGU account by the same amount.

7.8. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as Chairman of the Local
Development Council: (a) include only in the LGU’s Annual Investment Plan
the projects which are permanent or capital in nature so that utilization of the
20% Development Fund will be for infrastructure projects which will
contribute to long-term socio-economic progress of the Municipality; and (b)
review the contents of previous AIP and recommend to the LDC revision or
possible re-appropriations to other projects those in the AIP which are no
longer necessary and viable, so that all projects will be implemented during
the year.

8.5. We recommended that the Property Officer, with the assistance of the
Municipal DRRM Officer, shall assist in the conduct of actual physical count
of properties procured out of LDRRMF and prepare the Inventory List
pertaining thereto including the identification of the persons accountable
thereof.

8.6. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain an updated
subsidiary ledgers on DRRM equipment and reconcile said records monthly
with the records of the MDRRMO.

9.12. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
9.12.1. review RPT/SET collections of prior years up to CY 2019 to
determine the shares of each Barangay of LGU-Cabucgayan and the
Provincial share;
9.12.2. prepare immediately the Disbursement Vouchers for the release of
prior years’ payables on RPT/SET shares to the recipient barangays
and to the Province;
9.12.3. prepare a monthly schedule showing the amount of the RPT/SET
payable to the Province and each barangay and record in individual
subsidiary ledger; and
9.12.4. prepare, within five (5) days after the end of each quarter, the
Disbursement Vouchers for the release of the RPT/SET shares to
the recipient barangays and to the Province.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record in the Infrastructure
Asset accounts the public infrastructure projects completed prior to CY
2013, compute and record the depreciation thereon in order to present an
accurate balance of PPEs.

We recommended the following courses of actions:
2.8.1 The Inventory Committee conduct a physical inventory of local road
networks and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Local
Road Network (RPCLRN);
2.8.2 The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer maintain the
required Local Road Network Ledger Cards and its corresponding
Property Cards, respectively;
2.8.3 The Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant and the
Municipal Treasurer with the complete description and cost
segregation of road components for road projects.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant review the composition of the
Construction in Progress account to determine completed projects which has
not been transferred to its corresponding asset account in the Property, Plant
and Equipment and prepare the Journal Entry to reflect the accurate
balances of the projects in the said accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer: (a) submit the lacking DVs
totaling P5,748,194.67 within one month from receipt of this AOM to avoid
sanctions for non-rendition of accounts, and; (b) submit on or before the 5th
day of each month to the Municipal Accountant all paid disbursement
vouchers and other financial transactions for recording purposes.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant: a) demand, in writing,
from the Municipal Treasurer, the submission of disbursements vouchers
and reports, if not submitted within the prescribed period; (b) record
immediately in the books all transactions of the LGU within the period
mandated by regulations; (c) submit the lacking DVs and its supporting
documents; and (d) prepare a separate Bank Reconciliation Statement for
each bank account of the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Treasurer: (a) reconcile the differences in the balances of the Due to NGA
account per GL, SL and per bank and the Utilization Report; and (b)
maintain an accurate Subsidiary Ledger on the Due to NGA account per
source agency and per project to facilitate verification and monitoring of
each fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer obtain cash advances for
salaries, wages and allowances and release the same only upon rendition of
full services by the municipal officials.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor stop the grant of cash
advances to the Municipal Treasurer for salaries, wages and allowances
before services are rendered and bonuses before the DBM-prescribed dates.

We recommended that the designated Disbursing Officer maintain a cash book
for her cash advances for special operating expenses of the Municipality and
to liquidate her previous cash advances before obtaining additional funds.

We recommended that the OIC Municipal Treasurer initiate the preparation
of a Contract of Lease with the stallholders, to be renewed every year. Also,
maintain records of payments by each stallholder to monitor the collections
from the market stalls.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer transfer the actual cash on
the unexpended balance of LDRRMF for CY 2018 amounting to
P1,580,675.69 to the Special Trust Fund maintained for the purpose. We
also recommend that the Municipal Accountant monitor the LDRRMF
balances so that the transfer to the Trust Fund can be done on or before the
last day of each year.

We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer, the Municipal
Accountant and the MDRRMO appropriate and utilize the LDRRMF only
for disaster-preparedness and mitigation programs, projects and activities to
avoid disallowances in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, the GFPS ExeCom and GAD
TWG submit the necessary documents to prove how the commodities worth
P1.5 million for the Self-Help Livelihood Program were distributed and how
such intervention resolved the identified GAD issues. This is without
prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Disallowance if warranted.

We also recommended that the GFPS ExeCom and GAD TWG assist in the
preparation of GAD Accomplishment Report and use the HGDG tool in
attributing GAD related PPAs to come up with a realistic GAD
Accomplishment Report.

1. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor mandate the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical count of the properties of the Municipality to confirm the reported PPEs in the books.
2. review previous procurement data about the properties;
3. prepare and maintain PPE Ledger Cards, and to prepare Property Cards for every property procured by the LGU and reconcile his records with Accounting Records monthly.
4. identify and determine the discrepancies between the RPCPPE and Accounting Records.
5. Lastly, we recommended that the designated Property Officer prepare and maintain Property Cards for every property procured by the LGU and reconcile his records with Accounting Records monthly.
observations.managementAction: Inventory Committee had conducted a complete physical inventory of the Municipality PPE Copies of procurement data on some of the PPE were already obtained. PPE Ledger Cards started to prepare and maintain PPE Cards, and to prepare Property Cards for every property procured by the LGU and reconcile his records with Accounting Records monthly. Discrepancies between RPCPPE and Accounting records were partially identified. Property Cards started to prepare and maintain Property Cards for every property procured by the LGU and reconcile his records monthly.

observations.recommendation:
6. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor send a letter to Landbank requesting copies of the MOA and other documents pertaining to the donation and the disbursements on the construction of the Civic Center.
7. record the amount downloaded by the donor agency and the total cost of the Civic Center to the corresponding PPE account of the LGU once the documents pertaining thereto has been provided by Landbank;
8. submit to COA copies of the MOA and other documents on the donation and the disbursements on the construction of the Civic Center for verification and evaluation of the propriety of the transaction.
9. Lastly, we recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee submit the explanations why the copies of the bidding documents were not retained by BAC Secretariat considering that the bidding was conducted by the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Copies of MOA and other documents on the Construction of Civic Center were secured. Recorded per JEV 09-009 the total cost of the Civic Center to the corresponding PPE account of the LGU once the documents pertaining thereto has been provided by Landbank; Copies of MOA and other documents on the donation and the disbursements on the construction of Civic Center were submitted to COA. Explanation was submitted by the BAC for non-retention of the documents when the bidding was conducted.

observations.recommendation:
10. prepare the necessary adjusting entries to rectify the errors affecting the Cash-in-Bank account to avoid its repetitive inclusion in subsequent BRS, and communicate with the bank any errors made by them, if any.
11. prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement with accurate, complete and specific details on the reconciling items for prompt validation and verification in the source documents. We also recommended that
12. furnish the Municipal Accountant with complete copies of deposit slips and any notices of fund transfers to and from the LGU depository accounts, and
13. submit all disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents monthly to the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all transactions involving reduction of cash of the LGU are recorded by the Accounting Unit.
observations.managementAction: Bank deposits were constantly validated and submitted to the Municipal Accountant. Some of the documents were still lacking and were not yet recorded in the books. The OIC Municipal Treasurer prepared notices to the payees for the existence of the checks and determine the status if eligible for replacement. The OIC Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant submitted the lacking one hundred sixteen (116) DVs and its supporting documents, in addition to the unsubmitted DVs which were already paid by the bank to avoid the issuance of audit suspensions. The Municipal Accountant recorded the transactions in the books.

observations.recommendation:
14. We recommended that the OIC Municipal Treasurer send written notices to the payees and determine the status of the check and determine the status if eligible for replacement.
15. We also recommended that the OIC Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entries upon the cancellation of the check to correct the Cash in Bank account and other affected accounts.
observations.managementAction: Written notices were sent to the payees for the existence of the check and determine the status of the existence of the check. Necessary adjustments were made. The OIC Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant implemented the required adjustments.

observations.recommendation: 16. We recommended that the OIC Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant submit the lacking one hundred sixteen (116) DVs and its supporting documents, in addition to the unsubmitted DVs which were already paid by the bank to avoid the issuance of audit suspensions. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the transactions in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: Except for the one hundred sixteen (116) vouchers, one hundred sixteen (116) DVs were already paid by the bank to avoid the issuance of audit suspensions. The Municipal Accountant recorded the transactions in the books.

observations.recommendation:
17. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record in the books of the LGU all funds received from NGAs including the disbursements thereof. We recommended further that accounting records for these funds should be kept, maintained and updated.
18. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor designate a specific official to secure Memorandum of Agreements with the funding agencies for verification and validation of the programs/projects/activities undertaken by the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Differences still exist between the balance per books and per bank. A person were designated to secure the MOA and other documents from funding agencies for verification and validation of the programs/projects/activities undertaken by the LGU.

observations.recommendation:
19. We recommended that the designated water manager of Water Works System furnish the Accountant the monthly summary of water billings prepared and served to consumers. prepare and maintain an updated individual ledger cards for each consumer and prepare a monthly report of consumers with unpaid balances for comparison and reconciliation with the accounting records.
20. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant record as Receivables the amount of P2,259,581.77 for Water Supply services rendered and all water bills subsequently issued thereafter, based on the monthly Summary of Water Bills submitted by the Municipal Treasurer. Also, maintain an updated subsidiary ledger for each consumer to support the amount of receivables in the financial statements.
21. Further, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer intensify its collection of all billings made especially prior years' billings to defray recurring operating expenses of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Summary of water billings were forwarded to the Municipal Accountant. Receivables from the water billings were recorded. Collections on water billings were intensified.

observations.recommendation: 22. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review and reconcile the taxes withheld from suppliers, contractors and employees against the taxes remitted to ascertain the correctness of the Due to BIR account. Also, to ensure that DVs with withholding taxes are immediately recorded within the same transaction month and keep track of its remittances to avoid negative balances of the account in the books.
observations.managementAction: Records on Due to BIR were reconciled between the withheld and remittances.

observations.recommendation:
23. We recommended that all identified programs, projects and activities in the Annual Investment Plan under the 20% Development Fund are non-capital which are investment in nature so as to maximize the use of the 20% Development Fund and provide the citizens of the municipality with the benefits that can be derived from the projects.
24. We also recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant appropriate and utilize all the 20% Development Funds for purposes that are outlined in DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-01 dated February 22, 2017 and those identified in the AIP.
observations.managementAction: All identified programs, projects and activities in the AIP under the 20% Development Fund were all identified as non-capital expenditures which were equivalent to programs and projects in the 20% Development Fund. Utilization made under the 20% Development Funds were all identified in the AIP.

observations.recommendation:
25. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor implement strictly the identified GAD activities in the GAD Plan and utilize the funds in accordance with the legal basis. Also, stop the grant of allowances without legal basis.
26. We also recommended that the designated GAD Focal Person coordinate with different department heads to identify activities that would address valid gender issues for inclusion in the GAD plan.
27. We likewise recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Budget Officer maintain a registry to monitor disbursements of GAD funds to ascertain whether these are within the authorized appropriations for GAD and refrain from using GAD budget on expenditures which are not GAD related.
observations.managementAction: GAD activities were identified. Coordination was undertaken. Registries on Real Property Taxpayers were created. Some GAD activities were implemented.

observations.recommendation:
32. We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer designate a personnel to maintain and update the Index Cards of Real Property Taxpayers and ensure that demand letters are sent to delinquent taxpayers based on the report of the Municipal Accountant.
33. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the list of delinquent Real Property Taxpayers based on the Real Property Tax Assessment Roll submitted by the Municipal Assessor and the monthly Report of Collections from the Municipal Treasurer to support the balance of RPT Receivables account in the books.
34. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor institute legal remedies and develop other feasible scheme for strategies to enhance the collection of delinquent taxes from taxpayers.
observations.managementAction: Index Cards on Real Property Taxpayers were created. List of delinquent Real Property Taxpayers were prepared. Strategies for collection of delinquents were being developed.

observations.recommendation: 35. We recommended that the Local School Board incorporate in its budget programs and activities intended for Early Childhood Care and Development.
observations.managementAction: ECCD activities were incorporated in the budget programs and in the budget.

observations.recommendation:
36. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as the Chairman of the Local Council for the Protection of Children (LCPC), require the Council members to prepare the following documents for inclusion in the AIP and Annual Budget: a.) LCPC Annual Work and Financial Plan prioritized according to needs for integration in the local development plan; b.) Local Development Plan for Children (LDPC), and; c.) Local Investment Plan for Children (LCC).
37. We also recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer ensure that the total funds appropriated for programs, projects and activities to be undertaken by the different departments of the LGU which are attributed for the strengthening of LCPC and children’s rights protection should be equivalent to one percent (1%) of the IRA of the current year.
observations.managementAction: All LCPC’s required reports were prepared for inclusion in the AIP. Attributable programs, projects and activities were identified and were equivalent to one percent of the IRA.

observations.recommendation: 38. We recommend that the agency submit to the Audit Team all contract for the CY 2018 procurement of goods and infrastructure within five (5) days after perfection to facilitate timely review as to regularity of the procurement process and detect deficiencies prior to final payment.
observations.managementAction: Still not all contract for CY 2018 procurement of goods and infrastructure were submitted within five (5) days after perfection. Some contracts were submitted to COA for review.

observations.recommendation: 39. We recommend that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board strictly implement the establishment of Materials Recovery Facility (MRF) in every barangay or cluster of barangays pursuant to the provision of RA 9003.
observations.managementAction: Materials Recovery Facility were implemented in every barangay or cluster of barangays as required.

observations.recommendation: 40. We recommend that the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer submit immediately for audit the agency’s accounts and all financial records. Also, comply with the submission of accounts within the deadline set under Section 347 of RA 7160, Section 100 of PD 1445 and other pertinent laws, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: All accounts were submitted immediately for audit. The agency complied with the submission of accounts within the prescribed deadlines.

observations.recommendation: 41. Strictly require the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to conduct reconciliations of cash vault balances per cashbooks and ledgers at least monthly, the former to likewise comply with weekly turn-over of books of documents as well as consummated transactions to make the updating of records by the former. This has to be succeeded with recording of adjustments by the official whose records are considered erroneous on the basis of amount reflected per source documents like disbursement vouchers and payrolls with their supporting documents slips and their corresponding reports.
observations.managementAction: Both the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant started to conduct a reconciliation of the cashbook and records. The Municipal Treasurer and Accountant started to conduct a reconciliation of the cash in vault and implemented weekly turnover of books and records.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor mandate the Inventory
Committee to conduct a complete physical count of the properties of the
Municipality to confirm the reported PPEs in the books.
We also recommended that Municipal Accountant:
a) review previous procurement on PPE to obtain the necessary data
about the properties;
b) prepare and maintain PPE Ledger Cards, and
c) identify and determine the discrepancies between the RPCPPE and
Accounting Records.
Lastly, we recommended that the designated Property Officer prepare and
maintain Property Cards for every property procured by the LGU and reconcile his
records with Accounting Records monthly.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor send a letter to Landbank
requesting for copies of the MOA and other documents pertaining to the
Construction of the Civic Center.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a) record the amount downloaded by the donor agency and the total cost of
the Civic Center to the corresponding PPE account of the LGU once the
documents pertaining thereto has been provided by Landbank;
b) submit to COA copies of the MOA and other documents pertaining to
the donation and the disbursements on the construction of the Civic
Center for verification and evaluation of the propriety of the
transaction.
Lastly, we recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee submit
explanations why copies of the bidding documents were not retained by BAC
Secretariat considering that the bidding was conducted by the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a) prepare the necessary adjusting entries to rectify the errors affecting the Cash-
in-Bank account to avoid its repetitive inclusion in subsequent BRS, and
communicate with the bank any errors made by them, if any.
b) prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement with accurate, complete and
specific details on the reconciling items for prompt validation and verification
in the source documents.
We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
c) furnish the Municipal Accountant with complete copies of validated deposit
slips and any notices of fund transfers to and from the LGU depository
accounts, and
d) submit all disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents monthly to the
Municipal Accountant to ensure that all transactions involving reduction of
cash of the LGU are recorded by the Accounting Unit.

We recommended that the OIC Municipal Treasurer send written notices to
the payees on the existence of the check and determine its status if eligible for
replacement.
We also recommended that the OIC Municipal Accountant prepare the
necessary adjusting entries upon the cancellation of the check to correct the Cash in
Bank account and other affected accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant
submit the lacking one hundred sixteen (116) DVs and its supporting documents, in
addition to the unsubmitted DVs which were already paid by the bank to avoid the
issuance of audit suspensions. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant
record the transactions in the books of accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record in the books of the
LGU all funds received from NGAs including the disbursements thereof. We
recommended further that accounting records for these funds should be kept,
maintained and updated.
We also recommended the Municipal Mayor designate a specific official to
secure Memorandum of Agreements with the Work and Financial Plans from
funding agencies for verification and validation of the programs/projects/activities
undertaken by the LGU.

We recommended that the designated Manager of Water Works System
furnish the Accountant monthly with a summary of water billings prepared and
served to consumers. prepare and maintain an updated individual ledger cards for
each consumer and prepare a monthly report of consumers with unpaid balances
for comparison and reconciliation with the accounting records.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant record as Receivables
the amount of P2,259,581.77 for Water Supply services rendered and all water bills
subsequently issued thereafter, based on the monthly Summary of Water Bills
submitted by the Municipal Treasurer. Also, maintain an updated subsidiary
ledger for each consumer to support the amount of receivables in the financial
statements.
Further, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer intensify its
collection of all billings made especially prior years’ billings to defray recurring
operating expenses of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review and reconcile the
taxes withheld from suppliers, contractors and employees against the taxes remitted
to ascertain the correctness of the Due to BIR account balance. Also, to ensure that
DVs with withholding taxes are immediately recorded within the same transaction
month and keep track of its remittances to avoid negative balances of the account in
the books.

We recommended that the Municipal Development Council through the
Mayor as Chairman, include only in the Annual Investment Plan of the
Municipality projects with social, economic and environmental development
outcomes which are investment or capital in nature so as to maximize the use of the
20% Development Fund and provide the citizens of the municipality with the
benefits that can be derived from the projects.
We also recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal
Accountant appropriate and utilize the 20% Development Fund for purposes that
are outlined in DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-01 dated
February 22, 2017 and those identified in the AIP.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor implement strictly the GAD
activities identified in the approved GAD Plan and utilize the funds in accordance
with the purpose. Also, stop the grant of allowances without legal basis.
We also recommended that the designated GAD Focal Person coordinate
with other department heads in identifying activities that would address valid
gender issues for inclusion in the GAD Plan.
We likewise recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Budget
Officer maintain a registry to monitor disbursements of GAD funds to ascertain
whether these are within the authorized appropriations for GAD and refrain from
using GAD budget on expenditures which are not GAD related.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, together with the members of
the Sangguniang Bayan:
a) adopt a policy or guidelines to identify the specific officer and employee
authorized to claim mobile load, taking into consideration the
position/designation of the employee and its necessity and relevance to the
official’s function;
b) practice prudence and economy in the use of government funds for mobile
phone expenses by assessing the reasonableness of the amount to be granted
taking into consideration the financial status of the municipality;
c) avail of mobile postpaid plan which is more economical than prepaid load;
d) require the concerned officials to submit claims for mobile load on
reimbursement basis duly supported with Official Receipts on the load
expenses actually incurred.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer designate a personnel to
maintain and update the Index Cards of Real Property Taxpayers and ensure that
demand letters are sent to delinquent taxpayers based on the report of the
Municipal Accountant.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the list of
delinquent Real Property Taxpayers based on the Real Property Tax Assessment
Roll submitted by the Municipal Assessor and the monthly Report of Collections
from the Municipal Treasurer to support the balance of RPT Receivables account in
the books.
We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor institute legal remedies and
develop other feasible strategies to enhance collection of delinquent taxes from
taxpayers.

We recommended that the Local School Board Council incorporate in its
budget programs and activities intended for Early Childhood Care Development.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor as the Chairman of the Local
Council for the Protection of Children (LCPC), require the Council members to
prepare the following documents for inclusion in the Annual Investment Plan and
Annual Budget: a.) LCPC Annual Work and Financial Plan prioritized according to
needs for integration in the local development plan; b.) Local Development Plan for
Children (LDPC), and; c.) Local Investment Plan for Children (LCC).
We also recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer ensure that the total
funds appropriated for programs, projects and activities to be undertaken by the
different departments of the LGU which are attributed for the strengthening of
LCPC and children’s rights protection should be equivalent to one percent (1%) of
the IRA of the current year.

observations.managementAction: The related liquidation reports were submitted to the Municipal Accountant on all funds received from NGAs.
observations.reason: They are still retrieving all submitted documents related to the fund transferred from National Agencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the CY Municipal Accountant record in the books all purchased goods under the Inventory accounts and recognize an expense only when issued to end-users duly supported with the Requisition and Issue Slips (RIS) and the Monthly Report of Supplies and Material Issued (MRSMI).
observations.managementAction: AAR Finding No. 2
observations.reason: They are retrieving all purchased goods under the Inventory accounts and recognize an expense only when issued to end-users duly supported with the Requisition and Issue Slips (RIS) and the Monthly Report of Supplies and Material Issued (MRSMI).

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor prepare the LDRRMF Investment Plan to identify the programs/projects/activities to be implemented by the LGU for each calendar year from its current year fund and Trust Fund. Also, prepare the Report on Utilization for submission to all concerned offices and in accordance with COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction: AAR Finding No. 3
observations.reason: Management conducted the required activities and submitted the DRRM reports for audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the agency submit to the Audit Team all perfected contracts for procurement of infrastructure within five (5) days after perfection to facilitate timely review as to regularity of the procurement process and detect deficiencies prior to final payment.
observations.managementAction: AAR Finding No. 3 of CY 2014 audit, but all perfected Contracts in CY 2016 were not submitted to the Audit Team for review within the prescribed period.
observations.reason: The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) had instructed the Secretariat to submit all perfected Contracts in CY 2017 instead of within the prescribed period of 5 days.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the following corrective measures: a.) the Municipal Mayor sign a contract of service with each JO worker indicating period covered, wages, place of assignment, duties and other conditions of their employment; b.) the HRMO to attach to the contracts the results of assessment of each worker’s qualifications to determine that the worker can comply with the stipulated functions required of him; c.) the HRMO furnish the Audit Team with copies of the JO contracts pursuant to COA Circular 2009-001 to determine the legality and propriety of JO services; d.) require each Department Head where a JO is assigned to submit proof on the necessity of the services of each JO worker and the reasonableness of funds spent for their wages.
observations.managementAction: AAR Finding No. 5
observations.reason: They implemented the corrective measures and submitted JO contracts and supporting documentation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor issue a directive to all drivers to fill-up completely and properly all driver’s trip tickets to monitor and justify the reasonableness of the agency’s fuel consumption.
observations.managementAction: AAR Finding No. 6
observations.reason: Drivers were instructed to properly complete trip tickets and monitoring was instituted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor stop the payment of goods through reimbursement and procure through the appropriate procurement process so that payment is made directly to the supplier through checks.
observations.managementAction: AAR Finding No. 7
observations.reason: Some barangays had difficulty to sustain the MRF and resorted to alternative measures; municipality established a MRF in Sitio Kanumay, Barangay Baso.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to strictly implement the establishment of Materials Recovery Facility in every barangay or cluster of barangays and to strictly implement the establishment of the MRF.
observations.managementAction: AAR Finding No. 8
observations.reason: Cash assistance in the amount of P10,000.00 were given to barangays for establishment but some barangays encountered problem sustaining the MRF and resorted to alternative measures.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the agency immediately submit the reports and supporting documents covering CY 2015 to enable timely audit and detection of deficiencies; comply with Section 347 of RA 7160, Section 100 of PD 1445 and other pertinent laws, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: AAR Finding No. 1
observations.reason: Management's submission of accounts and other reports were delayed; some supporting documents were on leave of absence of personnel in charge.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and the Accountant submit the lacking DVs and its supporting documents and record the transactions in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: AAR Finding No. 2; Reiterat of this report
observations.reason: Twenty-four (24) DVs totaling to P2,371,966.58 were already submitted and recorded in the books; fourteen (14) DVs totaling P746,050.40 were still unsubmitted.

observations.recommendation: In the conduct of inventory-taking, instruct the Head of the Inventory Committee to complete and submit the report thereon; Instruct the Property Officer/Inventory Committee and the Accountant to reconcile the records in the General Ledger/Subsidiary Ledger with the Inventory Report/Property records; Implement strictly the conduct of regular inventory-taking and timely submission of Inventory Report and submit the latter to the Audit Team within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: AAR Finding No. 1; Reiterat of this report
observations.reason: Management conducted some physical inventory (CY 2014) and started reconciliation, but records were still not fully reconciled and purchases from earlier years remained unrecorded with corresponding costs.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and the Accountant reconcile balances of cash vault with the cashbooks and ledgers at least weekly and ensure turn-over of documents as well as timely recording of adjustments to update records.
observations.managementAction: AAR Finding No. 1
observations.reason: Management reconciliations were occasionally done and some turn-over of records occurred, but discrepancies remained and some records were erroneous based on source documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management submit the reviewed and approved LDRRMFIP and strict compliance to the relevant law and rules governing the administration of the LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2015 Implemented.
observations.reason: Reiterated as Audit Observation No. 7 of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer monitor the bank accounts of
the LGU and provide documents to the Municipal Accountant on any funds
deposited to the LGU accounts from NGAs. Also, the Municipal Accountant record
in the books of the LGU all funds received from NGAs as well as its liquidations.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant record in the books all
purchased goods under the Inventory accounts and recognize an expense only when
issued to the end-users duly supported with the Requisition and Issue Slips (RIS)
and the Monthly Report of Supplies and Material Issued (MRSMI).

We recommend that the Municipal DRRMO prepare the LDRRMF
Investment Plan to identify the programs/projects/activities to be implemented by
the LGU for each calendar year from its current year LDRRMF and Trust Fund.
Also, prepare the Report on Utilization of DRRM monthly for submission to all
concerned offices and COA in accordance with COA Circular 2012-002 dated
September 12, 2012.

We recommend that the agency submit to the Audit Team all contracts for
the procurement of goods and infrastructure within five (5) days after perfection to
facilitate timely review as to regularity of the procurement process and detect
deficiencies prior to final payment.

We recommend the following corrective measures:

a.) the Municipal Mayor sign a contract of service with each JO workers
indicating therein the period covered by their services, the rate of wages,
place of assignment, duties and responsibilities and other conditions of their
employment;
b.) the HRMO to attach to the contracts the results of assessment of each
worker’s qualifications to determine that the worker can comply with the
stipulated functions required of him;
c.) the HRMO furnish the Audit Team with copies of the JO contracts pursuant
to COA Circular 2009-001 to determine the legality and propriety of JO
services;
d.) require each Department Head where a JO is assigned to submit proof on the
necessity of the services of each JO worker and the reasonableness of funds
spent for their wages pursuant to the mandates of law on effective, efficient
and economical use of government resources.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor issue a directive to all drivers to
fill-up completely and properly all necessary information in the driver’s trip tickets
to monitor justify the reasonableness of the agency’s fuel consumption.

We recommend that the Municipal Government stop payments through
reimbursement and procure needed supplies and materials through the appropriate
procurement process and pay directly to the supplier through check issuance.

We recommend that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board
implement strictly the establishment of Materials Recovery Facility in every
barangay or cluster of barangays pursuant to the provision of RA 9003.
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant, Treasurer submit immediately for audit the agency’s preparation of accounts and submission of supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2015 Implemented.
observations.reason: The delay was partly caused by the late submission of reports in the Treasury. Also, personnel in the charge in the agency’s preparation of accounts and submission of supporting documents was on leave of absence.

observations.recommendation: We recommend for the CY 2015 Mayor to require the Treasurer and Accountant to submit the corresponding supporting documents for all Disbursement Vouchers (DVs) and all supporting documents covering CY 2010, 2013, 2014 and 2015 transactions of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2015 has submitted all the (37) Disbursement Vouchers (DVs) and all supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management to strictly adhere the required payment of goods and services by checks directly to payee except for small value items that could be paid out of petty cash fund. Stop the practice of reimbursement and strict adherence to the procurement law.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2015 now stopped their procurement payment of goods and services through reimbursement and submit the corresponding supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend for coursing of all common-use procurements to the Procurement Service and if without the required stocks, the LGU may resort to alternative mode of procurement in accordance with Rule XVI of the revised IRR of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2015 now purchase common-use supplies to the Procurement Service (PS) and if without stocks the LGU may resort to alternative mode of procurement with the required Certificate of Non-Availability of Stocks issued by the Procurement Service.

observations.recommendation: Regardless of urgency of payment, strictly adhere to the payment of checks directly to payees except for small value items that could be paid out of petty cash fund. Implement strictly the procurement law in every procurement of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2015 has stop incurring such expenses and will strictly observe prudence at all times in the disbursement of government funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management to submit at the beginning of the year a list of all on-going projects/programs/activities (PPAs) and those yet to be implemented during the year to preclude disallowances, in consonance with COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2015 management has submitted the on-going programs/projects/activities for CY 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommend conduct of inventory-taking on all components of the PPE and submit the reports thereon and reconcile the records in the General Ledger/Subsidiary Ledger with the Inventory Report/Property records.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2015 has conducted inventory taking on all PPE and has submitted the report to the audit team.
observations.reason: In consonance therewith, management proposed the undertaking of courses of actions and submitted the report to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management to submit the reviewed and approved GAD Plan and Budget and its corresponding Accomplishment Report for the Auditors to evaluate whether programs and activities of the agency were gender responsive and complaint.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2015 has submitted the approved Gender and Development Plan and Budget and its Accomplishment Report for CY 2016 and its accomplishment report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the prudence at all times in the disbursement of government funds to avoid the incurrence of irregular and excessive expenditures and that seminars/conferences conducted by local officials/authorities in the Province shall be held within its vicinity.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2015 had stop incurring the same expenses for CY 2016.
observations.reason: Careful scrutiny of any travel claims be made before granting payments to prevent misuse of public funds and to avoid disallowances.

observations.recommendation: Submit all perfected contracts/purchase orders in accordance with COA Circular 2009-001.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2014 Implemented.
observations.reason: Reiterated as Audit Observation no. 8 of this report.

observations.recommendation: Strictly require the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to reconcile 2010 accounts and conduct reconciliations at least monthly, and to comply with weekly turn‑over of documents as well as reports covering transactions to make possible the updating of records by the former.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2015 Management reconciliations occasionally came up with the same records. The Municipal Accountant’s Record and Municipal Treasurer’s Record lack thorough reconciliation.
observations.reason: Management lack thorough reconciliation between the Municipal Accountant’s Record and the Municipal Treasurer’s Record.
